items.caption_text:
Welcome to my new series: A Winner With the Whole Family.

A series where I share food that genuinely excites all age groups.
How do I know? Well, I’ve been cooking like this since my boys were toddlers and let me tell you, the feedback has been brutal at times. Now they’re 23 and 17, I’ve experienced every stage of feeding a family and, fortunately, they’re now far more appreciative!
Today I’m making chicken meatballs with lemon, Parmesan, garlic and herbs. These tasty little bites are brilliantly versatile, and I should say, it’s well worth making a double batch as they freeze really well raw.
Delicious served in a rich tomato sauce with pasta, tucked into a wrap or flatbread, or shaped into burger sized patties and served in buns. They also make a brilliant packed lunch bite cold the next day.
So, like I said, make more than you think, because these are going to be a hit with the whole family.

Chicken Meatballs with Parmesan Lemon Garlic & Herbs 

Serves 4
INGREDIENTS 

Meatballs
500g chicken mince
2 banana shallots or 1 small onion -finely chopped 
2 -3 cloves garlic - crushed 
30-40 grams of finely chopped fresh herbs - I used 2/3 flat leaf parsley 1/3 basil 
1 heaped tablespoon breadcrumbs 
2 tablespoon grated Parmesan 
Zest of 1/2 lemon 
1 egg
Salt & Pepper 

Tomato sauce 
1 medium red onion - finely chopped 
5 cloves garlic - crushed 
1 tin of good quality tomatoes or passata

METHOD 

Mix the meatball ingredients together thoroughly and roll into balls (slightly smaller than golf balls) keep in the fridge until needed. If you are making burgers then form into 4 patties. 

If you want to make the sauce -
Sauté the onion until soft and sweet. Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute before adding the tomatoes. Season generously, bring to a gentle simmer, lid on a cook on a low heat for 20 minutes.

Season the meatballs with salt and pepper. Pan fry the meatballs until golden on each side, transfer to the sauce to cook through. For burgers pan fry on each side until golden finishing in the oven for 10 ish minutes at 200 degree Celsius or until cooked through. 
#meatballs #family #kidsfood

items.caption_text:
Caramelised Peach  Burrata & Parma Ham Salad
I am loving this beautiful weather so I thought I’d share this gorgeous recipe. 
Over the years I’ve made various versions of this DELICIOUS salad. Today I finished it with salsa verde which was 👌 superb! It also works well with a good homemade pesto. 

Serves 2 as a main - 4 as a side
INGREDIENTS 
2-3 peaches or nectarines - halved and stone removed 
6 slices or Parma ham
1 ball of burrata - mine was smoked 
1-2 hearts of romaine lettuce 

Salsa verde 
1 - 2 cloves garlic 
4 anchovies 
1 teaspoon Dijon mustard 
2-3 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil 
Juice 1/2 lemon 
1 teaspoons honey
Black pepper 
1 small bunch of flat leaf parsley 
1 small bunch coriander 

METHOD 

Blitz the salsa verde ingredients together in a mini chopper loosening with extra olive oil if needed and set aside.

Add a small amount of Olive oil to the pan. Season the peaches and pan fry on a medium heat until golden. Plate up the salad leaves, tear the burrata, add Parma ham and the peaches. Drizzle with the salsa verde and a little extra olive oil if you wish. Season with salt and pepper and serve. #salad #burrata #weekend

items.caption_text:
Tuna Tataki with Asian Chimichurri 

Welcome to my new series - kitchen gadgets. In this series I’m going to share a few bits that I love, earn their place in my kitchen and genuinely deliver.
Today we’re focusing on this @kenwoodworld mini chopper. Coming in at £25 (currently just under on Amazon) its not going to break the bank. 
I’ve had it for years and it started it’s life life puréeing small amounts of baby food. These days it’s perfect for knocking up delicious little sauces such as Chimichurri, pesto, salsa verde and more. Today I’ve used it in this 15 minute recipe, which could be an individual meal served with rice, noodles and green veg. Or when entertaining as a sharing platter.

Serves 4 as a main 6-8 as a starter 

INGREDIENTS 

Asian Chimichuri ingredients 
1 bunch coriander 
1 red chilli 
1 - 2 cloves garlic 
1 teaspoon grated ginger 
2 tablespoons sesame oil
1 tablespoons soy sauce 
1/2 tablespoons white wine vinegar 
1 teaspoon honey
Salt 
Pepper 

4 Yellowfin tuna steaks
3-4 tablespoons sesame seeds - I used black and white 
2-3 spring onions - sliced 
1 red chilli - sliced 
Salt and pepper 

METHOD 

Blitz all of the Chimichuri ingredients together in a mini chopper. Loosen with a little extra oil if needed. Check for seasoning.

Heat a large frying pan until smoking. 
Cover each tuna steak in oil, seasoning generously and sprinkle or dip into sesame seeds. Cook for 1-2 minutes on each side

Remove from the pan.  Slice with a very sharp knife, drizzle with Chimichuri and finish with extra chilli and spring onions.  #kitchengadgets #searedtuna #tataki

items.caption_text:
Chicken Milanese & Shaved Fennel Salad 

One of my absolute favourite things to eat is a Milanese. When I was younger my mum used to make it with a big pile of rich garlicky, tomato pasta.  Today I have served it with a citrusy shaved fennel which is lovely! 

Preparation time – 10 minutes
Cooking time – 20 minutes

Serves 2
2 chicken breasts - skin removed 
2 eggs
1 clove of garlic
80-100g dried breadcrumbs (panko works well)
1 tbsp Parmesan, grated
Salt and pepper
Groundnut oil, for shallow frying

For the salad 
1 fennel bulb - sliced thinly reserving the herby tops
1 tablespoon capers
1 shallot - finely diced 
1 handful rocket
Juice 1/2 lemon 
1 tablespoon olive oil 
Salt and pepper 

Slice the chicken breast horizontally but not all the way through and open up. It should resemble a heart. Place in between 2 pieces of parchment paper and bash with a mallet or rolling pin until as thin as possible. Approximately 1.5 cm 

Whisk the eggs and garlic together, then coat the chicken in the egg mixture. If you have time, leave it there for 10 minutes. Mix the breadcrumbs and Parmesan together, and then season the chicken generously. Coat the chicken in the breadcrumb mixture, making sure it’s completely covered. In a very large frying pan, heat 1 cm of oil. When hot and almost smoking, carefully place the veal and cook until golden on one side. Don’t be tempted to turn it too early and keep it still so it creates a golden crust. Cook for roughly 3-4 minutes on each side and serve immediately.

Tips
Don’t over crowd the pan. It’s better to do it in batches. Use more oil than you think.  #milan #chickenmilanese #salad

items.caption_text:
Birthday Bundt Cake

Another trip round the sun means one thing… cake.
Alright yes, technically I’m another year older too and honestly, that one flew by.
This is my go to celebration cake, one I’ve been making for years. A proper classic vanilla sponge that never lets you down. It works in just about any tin, and just as well as cupcakes too.
If you’re going to learn one sponge recipe, make it this one. It’s simple, reliable, and exactly what a celebration cake should be.

Serves 6-8
For the sponge
227g caster sugar
227g soft butter, at room temperature
4 eggs, at room temperature
227g self-raising flour
2 tsp vanilla paste

For the frosting
200g full-fat cream cheese or mascarpone 
100g soft butter
1 tsp vanilla paste
100g icing sugar

For the cake decoration 
Raspberries 
Blueberries 
Blackberries 
White chocolate buttons
Mini meringues

Preheat the oven to 160°C fan/180°C conventional.
Grease two sandwich cake tins, with removable bottoms.
Start by creaming the caster sugar and butter together in a mixer on
a high speed, until pale and light. Beat the eggs and add a little at
a time to avoid curdling. When the egg is fully combined, stop the
mixer. Now carefully fold in the flour and vanilla paste – try not to
knock out the incorporated air. 

Grease the tin with butter and dust with flour. 
Cook in the oven for 35-40 minutes. 
Ensure the cake is fully cooked
by pricking with a cocktail stick – if it comes out clean, the cake is
cooked. Remove the cake from the tin and allow to cool. If using a Bundt tin the cover with a clean tea towel. The steam will help you remove the cake from the tin. 

If using 2 sandwich tins then cook for 25-30 minutes 

While the cakes are cooling, add the cream cheese or mascarpone butter and vanilla
paste to a bowl and beat with a wooden spoon. Add the icing sugar in
small increments.

Top with the frosting and decorate with fruit little meringues and white chocolate buttons. #birthdaycake #bakery #cakesofinstagram

items.caption_text:
White Linen

Move over Aperol, this is my new spritz of choice for the summer months. Similar to a Hugo Spritz this lovely cocktail is bursting with freshness thanks to the lime, cucumber, elderflower liqueur and gin.  Perfect for weekend entertaining and likely to get any party off to a good start. 
Have you tried them?

Makes 1
INGREDIENTS 
45ml gin
30mls lime juice 
15mls agave syrup 
15ml elderflower liqueur 
Cucumber ribbons 
2 inch piece of cucumber - roughly chopped 
Ice 
Fizzy water 

METHOD 

Shave a cucumber to get a few ribbons. Choose a glass and line the inside with cucumber 🥒 
Now freeze for at least an hour. 

Add ice to as shaker as well as gin, elderflower liqueur, agave syrup & lime juice. Give it a good shake. Add ice to a the glass, strain the cocktail into the glass and top with a little fizzy water to taste. #cocktails #cocktail #spritz

items.caption_text:
Greek Salad

As soon as the sun pokes its head out I am craving a lovely Greek salad. Simple delicious ingredients mixed together to create something truly lovely. This is how I make mine. 

Serves 4 as a side 2 as a main
INGREDIENTS 
1 cucumber - peeled, halved lengthways, seeds removed and sliced 
220g baby plum tomatoes 
3 tablespoons black olives 
1 block of feta - crumbled
1 small red onion - finely sliced 
Juice of 3/4’s lemon or 1 tablespoon red wine vinegar 
3-4 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil 
2 tablespoons chopped dill/coriander
Salt & pepper 

Mix together in a bowl and serve #greeksalad #saladbowl #healthy

items.caption_text:
Asian Sticky Pork Belly 

I’ve been wanting to make this for ages, and finally got the chance. It’s a simple two part recipe that I split over two days, which worked perfectly for my schedule.

First, the pork is gently simmered in a fragrant stock to tenderise it. Then it’s finished in a pan and glazed in a rich, sticky sauce.

Despite the two stages, it’s incredibly easy to make, and the results are absolutely gorgeous. Serve with jasmine rice and pak choi, and it pairs beautifully with a Gewürztraminer from Alsace.

Do give it a go when you get the chance.

Serves 4-6
INGREDIENTS 
800g pork belly strips - rind removed and chopped into 2.5 cm chunks 

Poaching liquid 
500-750ml chicken stock 
2 shallots - halved
4 cloves garlic - halved 
1 teaspoon pink pepper corns
3 slices ginger 
3 spring onions - each sliced 3 times
1 teaspoon salt 
2 teaspoon sugar 

Glaze
3 tablespoons honey
5 tablespoons soy sauce 
1 tablespoon sesame oil 
1 tablespoon mirin

2-3 teaspoons of sesame seeds 
3 spring onions - sliced 
1 red chilli - sliced 

METHOD 

Bring the poaching liquid to a gentle simmer and add the pork. Cook in the lowest heat with a lid on for 1.5 hours, spooning off any scum occasionally. 
Drain and pat dry. You can leave this in the fridge for a day before the next step. 

Add oil to a large frying pan. When hot carefully add the pork. Season and leave for 3 ish minutes on each side to get some colour and warm through. 
Mix together the glaze and pour over the pork. Let it bubble and reduce until sticky, being careful not to burn the marinade. Toss together to coat. Finishing with sesame seeds, spring onion and chilli. #porkbelly #wine #foodie

items.caption_text:
Peruvian Chicken Skewers with Aji Verde 

I’ve been meaning to make this amazing green Peruvian sauce for ages. I first tasted it when the Tanner brothers (Chris & James) @christanner07  @jamestanner01 made it for pub in the park a few years ago. Today I’ve made some gorgeous kebabs and finished it with the Aji Verde….believe me this is good stuff! 
Delicious cooked in the oven and perfect on the BBQ. Save this one for summer entertaining.

Serves 4
INGREDIENTS 
Marinade 
1 tablespoon olive oil 
2 tablespoons soy sauce 
1 tablespoon honey 
3 cloves garlic - crushed 
1 teaspoon grated ginger 
2 teaspoons smoked paprika 
1 level teaspoon ground cumin 
1/2 teaspoon chilli powder (optional)
Salt & Pepper

800g chicken thighs - skin and bones removed 
1 lime to serve 
Skewers 

Aji Verde - whizz together until smooth 
1 bunch coriander 
1-2 green chillies - seeds removed 
1-2 cloves garlic 
Juice 1/2 lime
1 tablespoon sour cream 
1 tablespoon olive oil 
1 tablespoon grated Parmesan 
1 teaspoon honey
Salt & Pepper 

METHOD 

Mix the marinade and add the chicken. Marinated in the fridge if you can. If not leave for 30 minutes.

Preheat the oven to 180 degrees Celsius fan, 200 degrees Celsius conventional.

Thread on to skewers and place on a lined baking sheet.  Season with salt and pepper and cook for 30 minutes turning once. 

Whiz together the sauce while the chicken is in the oven. Check for seasoning. This keeps in the fridge for 3 days. 

Remove from the oven and serve with lime wedges. 
#kebabs #peruvian #weekend

items.caption_text:
Oven Baked Prawn and
Chorizo Paella
For years, I assumed making paella was too tricky to try at home – until I started baking it in the oven. Paella is a celebration of bold flavours, and while purists might disagree about adding chorizo, I believe that when flavours work this well together,
it’s worth bending the rules.

Preparation time – 15 minutes
Cooking time – 50 minutes
Serves 4
2 small onions, finely chopped
Oil
5 cloves of garlic, crushed
200g chorizo, sliced
1 tbsp smoked paprika
300g paella rice
125ml white wine
1 litre fish stock or chicken stock, hot
Pinch of saffron (optional)
450-500g raw prawns (shell on or off, your
choice)
Olive oil
Salt and pepper
Chopped flat leaf parsley, to serve
Lemon wedges, to serve

You will need
1 paella pan, 36cm in diameter
Or
1 shallow casserole dish

Preheat the oven to 180°C fan/200°C conventional.
Place the pinch of saffron into the hot stock to infuse and leave
somewhere warm.
Fry the onion in a little oil, and when soft, add the garlic and chorizo.
Cook until the oils start releasing from the chorizo, then add the
smoked paprika and stir. Add the rice and mix well. Pour in the wine
and stir. Now add the hot stock including the saffron to the rice,
season generously and carefully stir.
Place it in the oven for 20 minutes.
Remove from the oven and nestle the prawns into the rice, then
return to the oven for 10 to 15 minutes. If it’s looking a little dry and
the rice isn’t cooked add a little extra stock and cook for a bit longer.
Finish with parsley and serve with lemon wedges.

Tip
The better your stock, the tastier the paella. An amazing fish or
seafood stock will really take this up a notch.
#paella #saturdaynight #wine

You will need
1 paella pan, 36cm in diameter
Or
1 shallow casserole dish

Preheat the oven to 180°C fan/200°C conventional.
Place the pinch of saffron into the hot stock to infuse and leave
somewhere warm.
Fry the onion in a little oil, and when soft, add the garlic and chorizo.
Cook until the oils start releasing from the chorizo, then add the
smoked paprika and stir. Add the rice and mix well. Pour in the wine
and stir. Now add the hot stock including the saffron to the rice,
season generously and carefully stir.
Place it in the oven for 20 minutes.
Remove from the oven and nestle the prawns into the rice, then
return to the oven for 10 to 15 minutes. If it’s looking a little dry and
the rice isn’t cooked add a little extra stock and cook for a bit longer.
Finish with parsley and serve with lemon wedges.

Tip
The better your stock, the tastier the paella. An amazing fish or
seafood stock will really take this up a notch.
#paella #saturdaynight #wine

items.caption_text:
The Best Potato Salad

If you have followed me for a while you will know that I’m not really a potato person however this is a recipe that changes everything. In order for it to be the best, there are a few rules which need to be followed! You can ditch your Maris pipers for this and go and get something waxy. Jersey Royals, Charlottes & Yukon Golds are my preference. Laced with spring onions, garlic, dill and capers and quite frankly a ton of mayonnaise, this potato salad is the best. 

INGREDIENTS 
500g new potatoes - boiled in salted water until cooked

The sauce - mix together in a bowl 
2 - 3 tablespoons mayonnaise 
1 clove garlic - crushed 
5 spring onions - finely chopped (shallots are an alternative)
1 tablespoons capers - drained 
1 tablespoon of chopped dill (chives, tarragon or chervil are an alternative)
Salt & pepper 

METHOD 

Cook the potatoes until tender and drain. When cool enough to handle slice into 3cm chunks. Mix the sauce ingredients together. Add the potatoes while warm. Stir and serve. #potato #salad #bbqsides

items.caption_text:
Chocolate Ganache Torte Cake

I made this a while ago and my goodness was it a treat! A rich indulgent and dense torte cake with decadent ganache and fresh raspberries. 
Let me know if this is something you’d enjoy?

INGREDIENTS 
200g dark chocolate 60% or it could be bitter 
220ml olive oil 
85g self raising flour 
85g plain flour 
1/4 teaspoon bicarbonate of soda 
400g caster sugar 
25g cocoa powder 
3 medium eggs
75ml butter milk

For the ganache 
300g dark chocolate 60%
200ml double cream 
1 tablespoons golden syrup 

300g raspberries to decorate 

METHOD 

Preheat the oven to 160 degrees Celsius fan. 180 degrees Celsius conventional 

Slowly melt the chocolate in a bowl over a little boiling water. 
Mix the flour, sugar, cocoa powder, bicarbonate of soda together in a bowl. 
Whisk the eggs and buttermilk in another. 
Now add the olive oil to the melted chocolate and allow to cool slightly before adding everything together until smooth. 

Split the mixture between two lined 8 inch loose bottom cake tins. Cook in the oven for 40-50 minutes until set but fudge like in the middle. The top will feel fairly hard and a little cracked. 
Allow to cool in the tins for 10 minutes before removing and cooling completely on a wire rack. 

For the ganache 
Warm the cream in a saucepan on a low heat and remove just before boiling point. 
Pour the cream over the chocolate. Stir until the chocolate has melted. Now add the golden syrup and stir. Allow the ganache to cool and thicken before decorating. 

Add 2 tablespoons of the ganache to one half of the cake. Top with the remaining half. Add the ganache to the top and finish with raspberries.

items.caption_text:
Asian Crab Cakes
These Asian-style crab cakes showcase the delicate sweetness of crab with a few Asian ingredients for the perfect balance. Golden and crisp on the outside with a delicious interior, they are lovely for a light supper, or as a gorgeous starter at any dinner party.

Preparation time – 10 minutes
Cooking time – 10 minutes
Serves 2, as a main, or 4, as a starter

200g white crab meat
½-1 red chilli, finely chopped
4 spring onions, finely chopped
1 tbsp finely chopped fresh coriander
1 clove of garlic, crushed
1 tsp grated ginger
1 egg
2-3 tbsp panko breadcrumbs
Olive oil

Salt and pepper Lime wedges, to serve In a bowl, combine all of the ingredients except the olive oil, salt, pepper and lime, adding extra panko breadcrumbs if it feels too wet. Using your hands, squeeze the mixture into 4 patties approximately 1
inch thick. On a medium-high heat, preheat a nonstick pan with a little olive oil. Season the crab cakes with salt and pepper, then add the crab cakes to the pan and cook until golden. Try not to move them around too much. Carefully turn with one quick movement – I find a silicone spatula works well here. When golden on both sides and hot all the way through, serve immediately.

Tip
It’s imperative to use a good nonstick pan here and don’t be tempted to turn the crab cakes prematurely. Give them a chance to colour and create a golden crust before turning once. #crab #cakes #dinner

items.caption_text:
Pesto & Prawn Pasta

I thought I’d reshare a recipe from last year which was inspired after a gorgeous week in Italy. We stayed with friends in Liguria which is known for its natural beauty, stunning bays, dramatic cliffs plunging into the sea, and also pesto. Whilst there we attended a pesto making class which was really interesting and lots of fun. This vibrant pesto can be added to pizza, focaccia, bruschetta and pasta. Obviously it be made without the prawns, but the addition of plump, sweet, seared prawns was a good one. 
I hope you like it 🇮🇹

Serves 2
INGREDIENTS 

Pesto ingredients 
1/2-1 clove garlic - sliced 
35g fresh basil leaves 
1 dessert spoon pine nuts
1-2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil - extra to finish 
1 tablespoon grated Parmesan 
1/4 teaspoon salt 

10-12 raw prawns - shell on or off your choice 
180 grams of dried pasta - I used Trofie

METHOD 

You can blitz the pesto ingredients in a mini chopper or in a pestle and mortar. If using the later. Start with basil, garlic, pine nuts and salt and pound until smooth ish. Continue with a circular mixing action to combine. Then add the Parmesan and olive oil. Add extra olive oil if necessary. 

Cook the pasta according to packet instructions in salted water. Reserving a coffee cup of pasta water. Pan fry the prawns for 5 minutes, or until pink in olive oil salt and pepper . 
Drain the pasta and combine with the pesto and a little of the cooking water. 
Top with the prawns. Finish with Parmesan, extra basil leaves and olive oil. 
Enjoy!
#pasta #pesto #prawns

items.caption_text:
Slow Roast Shoulder of Lamb

Here is a lovely way to cook shoulder of lamb. This recipe requires only a few minutes of prep. Then it’s over to the oven to work its magic, giving you the most succulent, sweet lamb, literally falling off the bone. The salsa verde takes it up a notch and balances the richness of the lamb beautifully.

INGREDIENTS 
1 shoulder of lamb 
4 small onions - sliced 
1 whole garlic bulb - top sliced off
 
To make a jus
1 teaspoon Red currant jelly 
100 ml red wine
 
Salsa verde
1 small bunch coriander 
1/2 small bunch fresh mint
1-2 cloves garlic 
4 anchovies
3-4 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil
1 tablespoon of red or white wine vinegar 
1-2 teaspoons Dijon mustard
1 teaspoon honey
Salt and pepper 
 
Preheat the oven to 200 degrees c conventional, 180 degrees c fan. 
 
Add onions and garlic to a roasting dish, drizzle with a little olive oil & season. Sit the lamb on top. You want the lamb to fit quite snug. Season with salt & pepper.
 
Cover with foil and cook in the oven for for 30 minutes. Now turn the heat down to 170 degrees c conventional, 150 degrees c fan for 6 hours, but anything up to 8 hours, removing the foil for the last 20 minutes. 
 
Whizz the salsa verde ingredients together and set aside.
 
Set the lamb aside on a warm plate and cover with foil. Spoon as much as the fat from the pan. Add the wine and bring to a simmer on the hob. Squeeze the soft garlic from the skin and mix into the sauce. Add the redcurrant jelly and scrape all of the flavour from the pan. For a smooth jus, then pass through a sieve. I don’t bother with this. #lamb #roast #roastdinner

items.caption_text:
Miso Glazed Side of Salmon 

Here is a beautiful example of easy, yet delicious entertaining. This side of salmon is gorgeously caramelised on the outside, yet sweet and succulent on the inside. 
Honestly divine! A lovely one to serve over the bank holiday weekend. 

Serves 4-6 
INGREDIENTS 
1kg side of salmon - skin attached
1 red chilli - sliced 
4 spring onions - sliced
1 thumb sized piece of ginger - peeled and cut into matchsticks 
Salt and pepper 

Miso Glaze
2 heaped teaspoons miso paste 
2 tablespoons runny honey 
2 tablespoons soy sauce 
3 teaspoons sesame oil 

METHOD 

Preheat the oven to 200 degrees Celsius fan. 220 degrees Celsius conventional.

Place the salmon on a lined baking sheet. Pat dry with kitchen paper. 

Mix together the miso glaze ingredients. Drizzle over the salmon. Top with ginger, spring onion and chilli. Season with salt and pepper and cook in the oven for 15 minutes. Allow to rest for 5 minutes. 
#salmon #side #miso

items.caption_text:
Shin of Beef Ragu

If you are looking to pimp up your spag bol, then you’ve come to the right place. This gorgeous ragu is a thing of beauty. Loaded with rich indulgent flavour, with the meat so soft and tender… I do wonder if you’ll ever make it any other way again?

Serves 2-4 

INGREDIENTS 
2 medium red onions or 6 banana shallots - chopped 
5 cloves garlic - finely chopped or crushed 
500g beef shin - chopped into 1 inch pieces (roughly)
300g chestnut mushrooms - chopped 
1 glass of red wine
1 tin of tomatoes or passata
400ml beef stock (I had leftover beef gravy and topped it up with a little hot water to loosen)

METHOD 

Sauté the onions until soft. Move the onions to one side and brown the beef seasoning everything generously. Add the garlic, mushrooms and red wine, stir and cook the alcohol off for a few minutes. Pour in the tomatoes/passata and stock, stir and bring to a very low simmer. Cover and cook on the lowest heat for a minimum of 2.5 hours or until the beef is soft and tender. Personally I’d cook it for a minimum of 3 hours really gently. Remove the lid for the last 30 minutes to thicken. If you have a slow cooker then this could be cooked all day on low. Check for seasoning and stir through pasta. 

Tip
If you cook it the day before and keep it in the fridge, then you’ll easily be able to remove any excess fat. Alternatively you can skim any oil using a spoon.

items.caption_text:
Roasted Romano Peppers with Burrata & Pinenuts 

I’ve  been making these Roasted Romano Peppers with Burrata and pine nuts for years and honestly it never disappoints. By roasting peppers they become rich, intense and wonderfully sweet. If you want to remove the skin then cover the peppers with a clean cloth for 5 minutes. The steam will enable you to peel the skin away easily. The combination of sweet peppers, creamy burrata with a little crunch from the pinenuts is one that will ensure you make this delicious side on repeat. 

Serves 4 as a side
INGREDIENTS
4 Romano peppers - cut in half lengthways, seeds removed 
1 ball burrata - drained 
2 tablespoons toasted pinenuts
2 handfuls of basil
Extra virgin olive oil 
Good balsamic vinegar (the reduction works best)
Salt and pepper 

METHOD 

Preheat the oven to 200 degrees Celsius fan. 220 degrees Celsius conventional 

Add the peppers to a large lined tray - skin side up. Don’t over crowd or they will steam rather than caramelise. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.

Roast in the oven for 20-30 minutes or until there are a few dark blisters appearing. 

Allow to cool and plate up. Peppers, torn burrata basil pinenuts drizzled in olive oil, balsamic, salt and pepper. #roastedpeppers #veg

items.caption_text:
Asparagus with prosciutto, burrata & pesto 

The best of British meets Italy with this gorgeous starter or light lunch. I can’t get enough asparagus when it’s in season so I thought I’d share another simple yet delicious way to enjoy this beautiful vegetable. A gorgeous combination which can be pulled together in minutes and is a complete joy to eat. Is this something you would enjoy?

Serves 2 as a light lunch 
INGREDIENTS 
8-10 king asparagus - washed and woody ends removed. I peeled the ends, but no need to do so.
1 ball Burrata 
6 slices of prosciutto
2-3 teaspoons fresh pesto 
Olive oil 
Basil leaves 

Here’s my favourite pesto recipe or you can buy from the fresh section in a supermarket…

Pistachio Pesto - blitz together until smooth 
80g fresh basil 
50g pistachio nuts - shells removed
30g Parmesan - grated
1-2 cloves garlic 
Juice 1/2 lemon 
1 teaspoon honey
150-200ml olive oil
Salt & Pepper

METHOD 

Blanch the asparagus in salted water for 2 minutes. Remove from the heat and drain. Add the asparagus, prosciutto & Burrata to a plate. Drizzle with the pesto and some olive oil. Finish with basil and black pepper. #burratta #asparagus

items.caption_text:
Spaghetti Alle Vongole 

20 years ago I was lucky enough to be sailing from Capri to Positano on the most stunning sailing boat called South Wind. As the sun was setting, the Italian captain of the boat quickly put on his apron and began cooking Spaghetti Vongole. The setting could not have been more perfect and the smell wafting up from the galley as we sipped Greco Di Tufo was sublime. I love how food can transport you back in an instant. Here is my version of that very dish. 

I’d love to hear your food stories?
Tell me in the comments…

Serves 4

INGREDIENTS 

350g dried spaghetti 
6 cloves garlic - finely chopped 
3-4 tablespoons olive oil 
1 chilli - finely chopped (optional)
400g tomatoes - roughly chopped 
500g small clams - cleaned and rinsed 
Small glass dry white wine ⠀
2 tbsp finely chopped fresh flatleaf parsley

METHOD 

Bring a large pan of water to the boil for the linguini. When boiling add salt and the pasta and cook until al dente.  Roughly 2 minutes less than the packet instructions.

Meanwhile...

Sauté the garlic in olive oil for a few minutes to release the flavour. Add the tomatoes and chilli, season generously and cook for 5 minutes. Pour in the wine and reduce to burn off the alcohol. Add the clams, stir, turn up the heat, place a lid on and cook for 5 minutes or until the clams have opened. Discard any that remain shut. 

Toss with the pasta, the herbs and a drop of the cooking water. Serve immediately.

If like me you are an asparagus lover, then you are going to want to give this one a go. Loaded with gorgeous flavour this makes the most delicious side. Keep your eyes peeled because early asparagus will be hitting the stores very soon. 

PS. Once asparagus season ends, try this recipe with Tenderstem broccoli.

Serves 4 as a side 
INGREDIENTS 
12-16 asparagus spears
5 spring onions - finely chopped 
1 red chilli - finely sliced 
Olive oil to cook

Sauce
2 cloves garlic - crushed 
1 tablespoon soy sauce 
3 teaspoons - honey 
1 tablespoon mirin
1/2 tablespoon sesame oil

METHOD 
Wash the asparagus and chop each into 3 pieces. 
Sauté the asparagus in olive oil for about 4 minutes stirring occasionally. Give them a little sprinkle of salt and add the spring onion and chilli after about a minute. When nearly cooked to your liking. Pour in the sauce and sizzle for one minute. Toss and serve immediately. #asparagus #sides #seasonal

items.caption_text:
Lemon Possets

I served these yesterday as part of our Mother’s Day meal mainly because they are so quick and easy to make. They feature in the dessert section of my book Simply Dinners. I’ll link it in stories. 
Bright, silky, and wonderfully simple, lemon possets are a timeless dessert that
highlight the fresh, tangy flavour of lemon and creamy vanilla. With just a few
ingredients, this velvety treat is both elegant and satisfying, a lovely finish to any meal.

Preparation time – 10 minutes
Cooking time – 5 minutes
Makes 6

600ml double cream
150g caster sugar
Juice of 2½ lemons
½ tsp vanilla paste or vanilla seeds
Zest of 1 lemon
2 tbsp caster sugar, to brûlée (optional)

Bring the cream to a very gentle simmer in a saucepan on the lowest heat. Add the sugar and lemon zest and dissolve, continuously stirring. Turn off the heat, then add the lemon juice and the vanilla and stir to combine. The acid in the lemons will naturally thicken the cream. Strain the mixture through a sieve into a jug if you want a silky smooth texture. Now pour into individual ramekins, glasses, coffee cups or lemon halves. Finish with lemon zest and leave to cool before placing in the fridge to set. If adding a brûlée topping, sprinkle each posset with sugar and melt the sugar with a blowtorch until golden. Allow to cool and harden before serving.

Tip
These possets can easily be made the day before, removed from the fridge 20 minutes before serving, and then brûléed for a crisp crust.

items.caption_text:
Harissa Shoulder of Lamb

So simple, but one of the loveliest things to eat and a lovely thing to serve this Mother’s Day. I’ve cooked shoulder of lamb in a variety of ways over the years.  I am particularly pleased with one of the ways in my new book - more about that another time. Sometimes I like to go really low and slow for 6 hours on a very gentle heat. Today I amped up the heat a little and was very happy with the results. The key here is to have a roasting tin in which the shoulder of lamb can fit snugly into, with enough depth to hold the juices that will be released. This way you not only benefit the rewards from roasting, but the lamb simmers slowly in its own juices. 

INGREDIENTS 
1 shoulder of lamb 
2 red onions - sliced 
4 cloves garlic - whole 

Marinade 
1 tbsp harissa
2 tsp olive oil
Juice of ½ a lemon
1-2 cloves of garlic, crushed

METHOD 
Preheat the oven to 200 degrees C 180 degrees C conventional.

Sit the lamb on a bed of onions and garlic cloves in a snug roasting tray. 

Mix the marinade ingredients together and coat the lamb thoroughly.
Leave for 30 minutes, or overnight.

Roast in the oven uncovered for 30 minutes to get colour and kickstart the process.  Remove from the oven and carefully cover tightly with foil. You may need a tea towel to protect your hands. Return to the oven and turn the heat down to 160 degrees Celsius fan 180 degrees Celsius conventional. Cook in the oven for 3 - 3.5 hours or until beautifully tender.  Allow to rest for 20 minutes before pulling apart with two forks.

items.caption_text:
How to Host a Dinner Party

If there’s one thing I’ve learnt over the years, it’s that hosting doesn’t need to be complicated. It’s not a competition, and you certainly don’t want your guests to feel the pressure when it’s their turn to host. The best dinner parties feel easy, full of laughter and relaxed.

Most of the recipes I share on social media and in my book Simply Dinners are created with exactly this in mind. Food that can be prepared ahead, finished calmly, and enjoyed together in a relaxed, comfortable atmosphere. As a host I want to be part of the evening, not stuck in the kitchen missing it all. I’m sure you feel the same.
For the full article go to substack…https://substack.com/@simplysecondhelpings/note/p-190400721?r=5rtve5&utm_medium=ios&utm_source=notes-share-action

items.caption_text:
Thai Inspired Chicken Meatballs 

I mentioned last week that I was making this recipe and lots of you requested the recipe. So here it is…
As I said the other day, chicken mince is incredibly diverse and takes of flavour beautifully.  Again, it’s one of the boys favourites - in fact my eldest son has taken a few to work as they are delicious cold too. Perfect made in advance and kept in the fridge or freezer until needed. Something you will definitely want to try. 

A few tips…
* Use fresh lemongrass not the dried variety and chop it up into tiny pieces, no more than 2mm
* The water content may vary in the chicken, so you may need extra brread crumbs if it feels too wet
* Make extra and freeze them in raw form or store cooked ones in the fridge for a few days - perfect for packed lunches cold, or reheated during the week 
* Use a nonstick pan and try not to move them too much in the beginning 

Serves 4
INGREDIENTS 
500g chicken mince
4 spring onions - finely sliced - I halved them lengthways the finely chopped 
1 blade of fresh lemon grass - tough outer leaves removed and finely chopped 
1 red chill - finely chopped 
2 cloves garlic - crushed 
1 teaspoon grated ginger 
1 tablespoons finely chopped coriander 
3 kaffir lime leaves - finely chopped 
1 egg
2-4 tablespoons breadcrumbs 
3 teaspoons soy sauce 
3 teaspoons sesame oil
2 teaspoons fish sauce 
2 teaspoons honey
Salt & pepper 

Chilli & mint to serve - optional 

METHOD 

Chop everything up as finely as possible particularly the lemon grass. 
Mix everything together in a bowl and roll into golf ball sized balls. If the mixture is too wet then add more breadcrumbs. Chill in the fridge to firm up.

Place a pan on the hob until hot. Sauté the meatballs on a medium to high heat in a nonstick pan. Try not to turn them prematurely to ensure a golden crust. Now keep turning until cooked through. You may need to turn the heat down. 

Serve with Jasmine rice and green veg. #meatballs #thaifood #homecooking

items.caption_text:
AD | Growing up mum didn’t cook like all the other 1980’s mums cooked. She was far more adventurous and she cooked everything from scratch.

I always put our love of Mediterranean food down to the Greek influence, but she said something the other day that made me curious to find out more. So, I sat down and signed up to Ancestry®️.

To celebrate Women’s History Month, I’ve been doing a bit of digging. I thought it would be interesting to discover more about my family history and heritage and then explore the cultural influences with food. Who knows what I’ll discover?!

Right now, Ancestry is releasing billions of records for Women’s History Month for you to explore for free, until 18th March. It’s a great chance to explore your own family history and see the story of the women who came before you.

*Registration is required to access the free records
#womenshistory #history #food

items.caption_text:
Steak & Mushroom Pie
It’s National Pie Week so I thought I’d remind you of this one. This tasty pie was made using shin of beef, which when cooked low and slow becomes tender and full of amazing flavour. The mushrooms add an earthiness, but also release liquid which helps with the cooking process. 
Serve with green vegetables of your choice and if you want to double carb…then a dollop of mashed potato. 
Enjoy!

Serves 4
INGREDIENTS 
600g beef shin - cut into cubes 
2 medium onions - finely chopped 
250g mushrooms - quartered
4 cloves garlic - crushed 
2 tablespoons plain flour 
500-600ml beef stock 
Salt & pepper 
1 roll puff pastry 
1 egg - beaten 

METHOD 
Season the beef and sear in a hot pan to get some colour but don’t cook through. Remove from the pan and set aside. Turn down the heat and add the onions with a little extra oil. Cook until golden. Return the beef and any juices, add the mushrooms, the garlic and mix. Sprinkle in the flour and coat the beef. Pour in 500ml of stock (saving the remaining in case it gets too dry) stir, cover with a lid and simmer on a very low heat for 2-2.5 hours or until tender. Be sure to stir from time to time. 
Spoon into a pie dish and allow to cool completely. I often make the filling the day ahead and chill overnight. 

Preheat the oven to 185 degrees C fan, 205 degrees C conventional.

Brush the rim of the dish with egg wash, top with the pastry. Seal with your thumbs and trim. Use the excess to make leaf shapes. Lightly score the pastry in a lattice shape. Decorate how you wish. Brush with egg wash. Pierce with a knife in the centre to allow steam to escape and cook for 30 minutes, or until golden. 

#pie #comfortfood #britishfood

items.caption_text:
Banana & Carrot Loaf 

No matter what age your kids are the mum worry doesn’t go away let me tell you! Even though both of mine tower above me, I still care about what they eat and hate the idea of them leaving for school or work on an empty stomach. This they can slice, pop in paper bag and head out of the door swiftly in the morning. (It wasn’t one I was actually going to share, so not my best video!)
However the loaf is delicious, so it was too good not to share. It’s hearty, isn’t overly sweet and also contains a good amount of fibre thanks to the bananas, carrot, sultanas and nuts. 
You can swap the pistachios for walnuts or sunflower seeds. Sultanas can be substituted with chopped up dried apricots or raisins. 
If you use a larger or shallower pan the loaf with cook quicker so bear that in mind. 

INGREDIENTS 
Wet ingredients 
3 ripe bananas - mashed 
1 large carrot - grated
80g butter - melted (then cooled slightly)You could add olive oil instead or a mixture of both 
2 small eggs - beaten 
1 teaspoon - vanilla paste 

Dry ingredients 
200g plain flour 
100g caster or brown sugar (or 150g if you prefer it sweeter)
1/2 teaspoon bicarbonate of soda 
A pinch of fine salt 
1 teaspoon cinnamon 
2 tablespoons sultanas 
1 tablespoon pistachios - optional 

A few thin slices of banana to decorate 

Loaf tin - mine was 8x4 approximately 
Liner or parchment paper 

METHOD 

Preheat the oven to 165 degrees Celsius fan, 185 degrees Celsius conventional 

Add all of the dry ingredients to a large bowl and mix to combine. 
Add all of the wet ingredients and mix until fully incorporated, but not for too long. Add to the lined loaf tin, top with banana slices and bake for 35-45 minutes or until golden and cooked through. Check with a skewer, when it’s clean when removed it’s cooked. 
Place on a wire rack to cool. 
#loaf #cake #breakfast
